title: procbomber-bench — sandbox for the procedural humanoid generator

stories.title: Flip KABOOM-CREW-CHARACTERS epic status to active
stories.summary: Epic was created in S100 with status 'planned'. This sprint is the first slice of that chain (mesh layer via the bench), so flip it to 'active'.
verification: backlog:check passes with no S101-touching errors.

stories.title: Engine: procedural mesh dispatcher inside MeshHandleRegistry
stories.summary: Add a project-agnostic procedural mesh path: any `MeshRenderer.mesh` value starting with `procedural:<key>` resolves through a per-renderer registry of `(seedHash) => BufferGeometry` builders, instead of the hardcoded primitive switch. Builders register at bootstrap. Cache by `<key>:<seedHash>` so repeat acquires reuse the geometry. (Engine work driven by the KABOOM-CREW-CHARACTERS chain — stays project-agnostic.)
verification: acceptance: createProceduralMeshRegistry exposes register/has/keys/cacheSize/resolve/invalidate/clear; isProceduralMeshRef + parseProceduralRef parse procedural:<key>[#<seed>] refs; mesh-handle-registry consults the procedural registry first for procedural: refs and falls through to primitives/external otherwise; ThreeRenderer constructs one + exposes proceduralMeshRegistry().
+12 unit tests in tests/unit/procedural-mesh-registry.test.ts; existing mesh-lifecycle-system tests still pass

stories.title: Doctor: report registered procedural mesh keys + cache size
stories.summary: When the renderer exposes its procedural mesh registry, engine doctor prints `Procedural mesh registry: N key(s), M cache entries`. Suppress the line when N=0 (no project registered any).
verification: acceptance: engine doctor prints 'Procedural mesh registry: N declared key(s) [...], M scene entity reference(s)' when N>0 or M>0; section is suppressed entirely otherwise; missing registrations are flagged ⚠ per key. Reframed from cache-size to a static scan of project source + scenes so doctor (offline) can see the surface.

engine/tools/doctor/project-doctor.ts: new ProceduralMeshesReport, summarizeProceduralMeshes walker (src/**/*.ts + bootstrap.ts + scenes/**/*.scene.json), formatProceduralMeshes output, missing-registration recommendation

stories.title: Pure humanoid mesh generator (head + torso + arms + legs)
stories.summary: Pure function `generateBomberMesh(spec) => BufferGeometry`. Six merged box parts (head, torso, left/right arm, left/right leg). Vertex colors from the spec's palette. Deterministic given a seed. Unit-tested for vertex count bounds + head-y > torso-y > leg-y invariant. Lives at `examples/procbomber-bench/src/generators/bomber-mesh.ts`.

stories.title: Eight named palettes + seeded variant picker
stories.summary: Palettes: sky, ember, mint, plum, sand, jade, rose, slate. Each is `{ head, torso, limbs, accent }` hex colors. `pickBomberPalette(seedHash, override?)` returns one. Pure, unit-tested.

stories.title: examples/procbomber-bench skeleton (scene, bootstrap, camera orbit)
stories.summary: Vite-compatible AGF project under `examples/procbomber-bench/` with project.json, template.json, performance-budget.json, a `start.scene.json` containing one ground plane + one bomber entity using `mesh: "procedural:procbomber"`, bootstrap that registers the generator + drives a slow camera orbit so the bomber rotates in view.

stories.title: DOM overlay: sliders + palette dropdown + reroll button
stories.summary: Plain HTML overlay (no UI framework) injected at bootstrap with: sliders for body-part size knobs (head, torso, arm-length, leg-length), a palette dropdown (8 options), a reroll button. Each control writes to the `ProceduralBomberSeed` ECS component on the bench's single bomber entity; the renderer re-acquires the procedural mesh on seed change.

stories.title: Animation switcher: idle-bob + walk-swing stub animations
stories.summary: DOM dropdown picks between {none, idle-bob, walk-swing}. A bench-local placeholder animation system reads `BenchAnimationState.kind` and drives Transform.position.y (bob) or arm/leg yaw (swing) per fixedUpdate. Idle-bob is sine on Y; walk-swing alternates limb yaw +/- 0.3 rad at 4Hz. These are stubs that prove the dropdown wires through the data path — the full S102 animation systems will replace them with proper limb-anchor animation.

stories.title: Playwright smoke: bench loads, every UI control responds
stories.summary: Playtest scenario under `examples/procbomber-bench/playtests/` that opens the page, asserts the bomber mesh is in the scene snapshot, ticks each slider + palette + animation dropdown via DOM events, captures one screenshot per state transition. Adds the bench to the preflight smoke run.

notes: User explicitly asked for a separate bench with sliders + animation toggle. Agent-first stays the default; this is an authorized human-UI exception driven by the need to iterate the generator visually before the polish bar is locked.
Engine work in AGF-PROCMESH-REGISTRY must stay project-agnostic — no procbomber-specific strings in engine/ paths.
GDP-009 (the full six animation ECS systems) and GDP-010 (procedural vocal synth) follow in later sprints. This sprint ships only the two stub animations needed to prove the bench dropdown.
